feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Xbox's new chief strategist is a metaverse true believer
entry.content: https://www.pcgamer.com/gaming-indu stry/matthew-ball-was-hired-to-help -fix-xbox-will-gamers-like-what-he- prescribes/ >A star in the games business Twitter circuit, investor and analyst Matthew Ball has made waves in the industry over recent years with his "State of Video Gaming" slideshows, which in some corners have become doctrinal explanations for gaming's 2020s woes. >The big-picture Xbox strategy hasn't been shared (and is surely still developing), but Ball's history suggests that Tim Sw

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Is this proof fromsoft has gone fullretard?
entry.content: Can't understand the logic behind the decision to make it: 1. A switch 2 exclusive 2. An extraction pvpve slop 3. A multiplayer focused game Gonna be honest, I did not enjoy elden ring as much as other people did, but even that is better than whatever liveservice slop they are cooking right now. I thought they learned their lesson with Nightreign but it seems not. Still really weird for them to do a full 180 on what made them popular and go full multiplayer now Also Myiazaki fuck you for not mak

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: What does it mean?
entry.content: I just woke up from a dream where I was on a date with Maisie Williams, and I had just brought her back to my room. We drunkenly dance for a little while then she takes her top off revealing breasts that look much larger than what I saw in her photos. Right as I cop a feel, Jerry Seinfeld bursts in my room like Kramer and steals the experience off of my MMO hotbar. I start to protest and he yells >WHAT ARE YOU TALKING ABOUT, THIS IS THE COSTANZA SHOW *laugh track* That's when I notice his head i

entry.title: /g/ Technology: So-called "artificial intelligence" is going to be the end of this world. Regula…
entry.content: So-called "artificial intelligence" is going to be the end of this world. Regular 15% increases in energy consumption are not sustainable, the planet will exhaust its natural resources in a few years, and will boil over from waste heat in a few decades. Yet unsophisticated shareholders continue to imbue these bullshit generators with romantic power, not realizing it's just machine learning algorithms under the hood that at best are only useful as pattern recognition tools, so mostly military and

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Software engineering is DEAD
entry.content: Everyone in this field is coping so hard right now, it's probably the most massive cope ever seen in the history of jobs. People denying reality right in front of them, saying that "AI won't replace SWEs" despite 4 years of constant layoffs with no end in sight. >inb4 muh economy >inb4 muh indians >inb4 muh-oversaturation >inb4 muh covid overhiring ALL COPES. With the exception of COVID, these issues existed 10 years ago when the market was thriving. And it's been like 5 fucking years since COVI

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >in the time period that I was there there was more of a variety of different pe…
entry.content: >in the time period that I was there there was more of a variety of different people of ideologies because there weren't a lot of places to talk about the stuff people were talking about on 4chan. It was many different boards but for example on the music board – because I was mostly on the music board – whenever somebody would come in and say something ridiculous or racist or sort of like offensive people would be like "go back to /pol/". Mostly the politics board was relegated to the psychos. B

entry.title: /int/ International: My country spiritually died in 1914-1918 and never recovered. The human, materia…
entry.content: My country spiritually died in 1914-1918 and never recovered. The human, material and moral cost of the war led to the defeat of 1940, which itself led to the humiliating loss of our colonial empire and jacobin forces doubling down on centralisation, which led to the death of most of our regional cultures and languages, accelerating our loss of collective identity. That cost can still be felt today in our collective nihilism made worse by the arrival of mass consumption in the 1950s.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Taking mom to a metal show
entry.content: So, my mom is a fan of a metal band but never saw any concert live. The band in question is coming to town later this month and she got us two tickets for golden circle. I'm quite experienced with metal crowds so I'm pretty sure it will suck for a 5'3 middle-age woman to be next to the moshpit or behind some tall guys not being able to see the stage. I want to ensure she has a good time and I'm thinking we should push for the front row or stand with our backs to the FOH. Any advice? I'm not disc

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Two of the top three games on Steam are porn games. These are the 3 most visible…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Are you into computers? Are you into computers son? Do you- do you work the comp…

Well, you know how the way the letters are all laid out over the place in the... *wiggles fingers* Well, do you know that they managed to work out, if we could go back thousands of years and change the alphabet so that it was in that order to begin with... entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Tomorrowverse: A DC:AMU Saga
entry.content: Instead of complaining about DC:CU's (Cinematic Universe) failings, we should appreciate more what we have in the DC:AU's (Animated Universe) Tomorrowverse movie run. It is ~10 movie's with rock solid storytelling fundamentals and takes that separate them nicely from tired live-action tropes, all culminating in a Crisis On Infinite Earths trilogy, which contests the M:CU's overall plot progression game in it's Infinity Saga quite nicely. In particular, reddit-style jokes (those cringey quips we

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: New funny stiller kino
entry.content: >The 2026 film Focker-in-Law follows Greg and Pam Focker's grown son, Henry, who brings his new girlfriend, Olivia (played by Ariana Grande), home to meet his eccentric family and reveals plans to propose. Olivia, a trained former FBI hostage negotiator, faces heavy scrutiny from ever-suspicious Jack Byrnes (Robert De Niro) and his infamous lie detector test. However, she surprises the family by expertly passing his tests and winning a coveted spot in the “circle of trust,” while hiding a few se

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What's the appeal behind not putting a subject title in your threads, /g/?
entry.content: Ewww! This looks so ugly! Look at how uncivilized these no-subject posters are! On the Internet, having a subject title is incredibly important if you want your readers to understand what you're trying to talk about. Imagine if Linux didn't have a name and instead all you got was "A monolithic approach to reimplementing MINIX's kernel API". Sure, it's descriptive, but your fucking project doesn't have a name! It's forgettable and makes it harder for people to want to respond or contribute to you

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Callahan & Witscher
entry.content: So these guys are experimental noise artists from the New York conceptual art/post-future scene. Each of them has a bunch of aliases (Rene Hell, Die Reihe), and they've both spent more than 20 years making really, really difficult-to-listen-to noise music. Their collaborative performance project—an overwhelmingly conceptual, hyper-self-aware exploration of the form, limitations, and meaning of art itself—gradually evolved until it ended up looking a lot like a band. The best part? It's all pop.
